source_packet.title: A New Policy Optimization Paradigm for LLM Reasoning
source_packet.audience: Readers who know policy gradients but have not studied off-policy reasoning-model training.
source_packet.central_question: Can one training signal respond to both trust-region drift and off-policy sampling without tuning PPO-style clip bounds?

media.caption: A Manim cascade from importance weights to normalized effective sample size.
Poster frame for the ESS cascade.
English captions for the ESS cascade.

paper_excerpts.text: The paper separates two problems often conflated by clipping: trust-region control limits policy drift, while off-policy correction accounts for samples produced by another behavior policy. It defines normalized ESS from importance weights and uses ESS in the P3O objective.

The reported experiments compare clip sensitivity, rollout temperatures, and BF16 training with FP8 rollout inference. P3O is reported as stable across these shifts while GRPO is sensitive in several settings.
Table 7 reports held-out pass@1. On AMC, P3O is 0.493 versus a 0.381±0.195 average for three GRPO clip settings; at FP8 iteration 30 it reports 0.529 for P3O and 0.029 for GRPO.

code_notes.text: The implementation masks padded ratios, aggregates sum(w), sum(w²), and count, computes normalized ESS, clips rho at ESS, detaches rho in the policy term, and scales behavioral KL by 1-ESS.

agent_input.claim_policy: Use exact values only when tied to a table or equation locator. Label qualitative figure readings as reported trends. Keep authors' claims distinct from pipeline interpretations.

concept_graph.thesis: ESS can act as one batch-level state variable that tightens importance weighting and strengthens a behavioral trust region as policy overlap falls.

nodes.label: Policy gradient
nodes.summary: Improves actions in proportion to advantage under the current policy.

nodes.label: Importance sampling
nodes.summary: Reweights behavior-policy samples by a current-to-old policy ratio.

nodes.label: PPO / GRPO clipping
nodes.summary: Uses fixed clip bounds as a practical control on large updates.

nodes.label: Normalized ESS
nodes.summary: Measures concentration of token-level importance weights on the valid batch.

nodes.label: P3O objective
nodes.summary: Caps detached policy weights at ESS and scales behavioral KL by one minus ESS.

edges.teaching_point: Sampling and optimization policies can differ.
A fixed clip is a control knob, not a measurement of overlap.
Weight concentration becomes a batch diagnostic.
Replace a fixed clip hyperparameter with a measured batch state.
One scalar drives two complementary responses.

unresolved: Whether one batch-level ESS is sufficient for heterogeneous token or sequence regimes remains open in the paper's discussion.

lesson_plan.title: When a batch tells the optimizer how far it has drifted
lesson_plan.promise: Start from the mismatch between rollout and learner policies, derive ESS as an overlap signal, then trace that signal through formula, code, and reported results.

sections.title: Two failures, one overloaded clip
sections.question: What exactly goes wrong when rollouts become stale?
sections.learning_goal: Separate trust-region drift from off-policy correction.
sections.misconception: Clipping is itself a direct measurement of distribution overlap.
sections.summary: Fixed bounds control update size, while stale samples create a distinct weighting problem.

sections.title: From score functions to batch adaptation
sections.question: Which prior idea does each P3O term inherit?
sections.learning_goal: Place P3O after policy gradients, importance sampling, and PPO/GRPO.
sections.misconception: P3O is an isolated formula with no recognizable ancestry.
sections.summary: P3O recombines familiar pieces around a measured batch state.

sections.title: Read the shape of the weights
sections.question: What does ESS notice that a fixed clip does not?
sections.learning_goal: Relate concentrated importance weights to low effective sample size.
sections.misconception: ESS is merely the physical batch size.
sections.summary: ESS falls when a few ratios dominate, even when token count stays fixed.

sections.title: One scalar, two control paths
sections.question: How does ESS alter the objective?
sections.learning_goal: Follow the bottom-up cascade from ratios to cap and KL weight.
sections.misconception: ESS only replaces the upper clip bound.
sections.summary: Low ESS simultaneously caps detached importance weights and increases behavioral KL.

sections.title: Equation to implementation
sections.question: Where do masking, detach, cap, and KL appear?
sections.learning_goal: Map formula operations to confirmed code lines.
sections.misconception: The executable loss is identical to a typeset equation without numerical safeguards.
sections.summary: The code handles padding, distributed aggregation, finite guards, and mixed precision around the core equation.

sections.title: Stress the distribution shift
sections.question: Where does the adaptive signal matter empirically?
sections.learning_goal: Read exact benchmark values alongside the paper's qualitative stability claims.
sections.misconception: One benchmark number proves universal stability.
sections.summary: The strongest evidence comes from varied clip, temperature, and rollout precision settings.

sections.title: What the demo must not overclaim
sections.question: Which conclusions remain conditional?
sections.learning_goal: Distinguish reported evidence, design interpretation, and open questions.
sections.summary: Batch granularity, tested regimes, and the unstable two-anchor extension bound the claim.

blocks.heading: A stale rollout creates two different questions
paragraphs: How far did the current policy move from the policy that produced these tokens?
How much should each off-policy token count when estimating the new gradient?

columns.label: Trust region
columns.question: How large is the update?
columns.answer: Penalize drift from the behavior policy.

blocks.prompt: If every importance ratio is 1, which problem disappears?
blocks.answer: The batch perfectly overlaps the behavior policy, so importance weights are uniform and normalized ESS is 1.

blocks.heading: The conceptual move
paragraphs: P3O does not discard policy-gradient or importance-sampling machinery. It asks the batch to report overlap, then uses that report where a fixed clip hyperparameter previously carried too much responsibility.

blocks.title: Same token count, different effective support
blocks.note: The second batch still has four tokens, but one token dominates its weighted estimate.

blocks.heading: ESS is a shape statistic
paragraphs: Scaling every ratio by the same positive constant leaves normalized ESS unchanged. What matters is concentration, not the raw scale or batch count alone.

blocks.title: Bottom-up animation cascade
blocks.caption: Ratios → weight concentration → ESS → two objective controls

steps.label: Measure
steps.meaning: Batch overlap becomes a scalar state.

steps.label: Cap
steps.meaning: Low overlap limits the detached policy weight.

steps.label: Restrain
steps.meaning: Low overlap strengthens the behavioral trust region.

blocks.heading: What the equation omits
paragraphs: The full implementation also masks padding before exponentiation, aggregates statistics across distributed ranks, guards non-finite values, and uses float32 or float64 at sensitive steps.

blocks.title: AMC pass@1, BF16 train + FP8 rollout at iteration 30

items.label: Clip sensitivity
items.finding: P3O matches or exceeds the best GRPO clip variant across the reported model families without a clip hyperparameter.

items.label: Temperature shift
items.finding: The paper reports P3O as comparatively stable while GRPO degrades under several changed rollout temperatures.

items.label: Precision mismatch
items.finding: With BF16 training and FP8 rollout inference, the paper reports late GRPO collapse and sustained P3O performance in the shown setting.

items.label: Granularity
items.detail: One normalized ESS summarizes an entire batch; heterogeneous sequences may merit finer signals.

items.label: Evidence boundary
items.detail: The experiments cover selected models, datasets, temperatures, rollout lengths, and precision regimes.

items.label: Extension instability
items.detail: The two-anchor extension collapses at temperature 1.2 in the appendix experiment, despite remaining stable at the default temperature.

items.label: Interpretation
items.detail: The pipeline's ‘one scalar, two paths’ framing is a teaching decomposition of the cited objective, not a new theorem.

blocks.prompt: Does low ESS prove that every token is bad data?
blocks.answer: No. It says the importance-weight distribution is concentrated at the chosen aggregation level; diagnosis and response are batch-level in this formulation.

appendix_entries.title: Source provenance
appendix_entries.body: Paper arXiv:2605.12380v1, 18 pages, PDF SHA-256 19b66e…15e. Code revision dfe85351…5752. All packaged media are hash checked during validation.

appendix_entries.title: Related-work map
appendix_entries.body: The teaching order separates policy-gradient ancestry, importance weighting, proximal clipping, ESS diagnostics, and the adopted P3O objective instead of presenting a flat citation list.

appendix_entries.title: ESS derivation note
appendix_entries.body: Normalized ESS is mean(ρ)² / mean(ρ²), equivalent to (Σρ)² / (n Σρ²). It lies in (0,1] for positive weights and reaches 1 for uniform weights.

appendix_entries.title: Formula-to-animation mapping
appendix_entries.body: Built-in Manim primitives cover bars and transforms; project-inherited formula walking handles symbolic focus; the ESS tradeoff gauge is newly authored. Candidate and unresolved mappings stay visibly labeled.

appendix_entries.title: Code mapping
appendix_entries.body: The committed snapshot records the exact upstream revision and original line ranges. The site renders selected lines only; the source packet retains repository and commit identity.

appendix_entries.title: Results reading guide
appendix_entries.body: Bars use exact Table 7 values. Figure summaries are labeled as authors' reported trends because the pipeline does not digitize curves into invented datapoints.

appendix_entries.title: Limitations and open questions
appendix_entries.body: The paper notes a possible limitation of one ESS signal and reports a temperature-sensitive failure of the two-anchor extension. Neither the site nor pipeline generalizes beyond the cited regimes.
